skill.name: video-enhancement
skill.description: Upscale and enhance video quality using AI via verging.ai. Supports local files and YouTube/Bilibili URLs with HD/4K output.
skill.summary: AI-powered video upscaling and enhancement with support for local files and streaming URLs.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate video enhancement tool that wraps the verging.ai API. Static analysis flagged shell commands (ffmpeg, curl, yt-dlp), env var access (VERGING_API_KEY), network calls to verging.ai, and temp directory usage. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: the shell commands are standard video processing tool invocations documented for instructional purposes, the API key access follows best practices (env var only), and network calls are to the documented verging.ai API. The combination heuristic is expected for a skill that processes media files via a cloud API. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell command execution via ffmpeg, curl, and yt-dlp
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs execution of shell commands including ffmpeg for video trimming, curl for API calls, and yt-dlp for video downloads. These are standard media processing tools invoked with documented parameters. While the execution pattern triggers static analysis, the commands are purposeful and scoped to the video enhancement workflow.

medium_findings.title: API key handling via environment variable
medium_findings.description: The skill accesses VERGING_API_KEY environment variable for authentication with the verging.ai API. This is the recommended secure pattern for credential management. The key is never hardcoded; users set it via export. Privacy section explicitly warns against exposing it in public repos.

low_findings.title: Network requests to verging.ai API endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to verging.ai/api/v1/* endpoints for authentication, video upload, job creation, and status polling. All requests are to the documented API of the service the skill is designed to interface with. No requests to suspicious or external third-party domains.

low_findings.title: Temp directory usage for video processing
low_findings.description: The skill uses /tmp/verging-video-enhancement/ as a working directory for trimmed video files. This is a standard ephemeral location for media processing. The skill notes temp files are cleaned up after use.

content.user_title: Enhance and upscale videos with AI
content.value_statement: Process raw or low-quality video files with AI-powered upscaling. This skill automates download, trim, upload, and enhancement for local files and streaming URLs.
actual_capabilities: Upscale video resolution to HD or 4K using AI processing
Denoise and sharpen video frames automatically
Download videos from YouTube and Bilibili URLs via yt-dlp
Trim videos to a maximum of 30 seconds using ffmpeg
Upload processed videos to verging.ai cloud for enhancement
Poll job status and return or download enhanced results
limitations: Maximum video duration is 30 seconds; longer videos are auto-trimmed
Requires a valid verging.ai API key with available credits
Depends on local installation of yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl
HD upscaling costs 5 credits per second; normal mode costs 3 credits per second

use_cases.title: Social media content creator
use_cases.description: Quickly upscale short clips for Instagram, TikTok, or YouTube Shorts without manual editing software.

use_cases.title: Video restoration hobbyist
use_cases.description: Enhance old or low-quality home videos to modern resolution standards with one command.

use_cases.title: Developer prototyping media tools
use_cases.description: Use the verging.ai API workflow as a reference for building video processing pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance a local video file
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage with a local file

prompt_templates.title: HD upscale with auto-download
prompt_templates.scenario: High-quality output saved to specific directory

prompt_templates.title: Trim and enhance a segment
prompt_templates.scenario: Extract and enhance a specific time range

output_examples.output: Job created successfully (ID: 123). Polling status... COMPLETED. Enhanced video available at: https://img.panpan8.com/video-enhance/result_123.mp4
Downloaded video to /tmp/verging-video-enhancement/input.mp4. Trimmed to 20s. Credits: 60. Job created (ID: 456). Result: https://img.panpan8.com/video-enhance/result_456.mp4

best_practices: Set VERGING_API_KEY as an environment variable rather than passing it inline in commands
Trim videos to the required segment before upscaling to minimize credit usage
Verify you have rights to process the media before uploading to the API
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in scripts or commit them to version control
Do not process videos longer than 30 seconds without trimming first; the API will reject them
Do not use JSON body for upload or job creation endpoints; both require form data with -F flags

faq.question: What tools need to be installed?
faq.answer: You need yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl available on your system PATH.

faq.question: How much does enhancement cost?
faq.answer: Normal mode costs 3 credits per second. HD mode costs 5 credits per second.

faq.question: Can I process videos longer than 30 seconds?
faq.answer: The API limits jobs to 30 seconds. Use --start and --end flags to trim longer videos first.

faq.question: Where do enhanced videos get saved?
faq.answer: By default the result URL is displayed. Use --download flag to save to your current directory or specify --output for a custom path.

faq.question: Is my API key safe?
faq.answer: The key is read from the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable. Never paste it in public repos or shared terminals.

faq.question: Which video sources are supported?
faq.answer: Local files of any common format plus YouTube and Bilibili URLs through yt-dlp.
